target.name: (+)-aplysiasecosterol A
target.motivation: Natural scarcity limits biological activity research; first asymmetric total synthesis.

success_criteria.free_text: Satisfactory yield and ee value on multiple gram scales.

key_disconnections.reasoning: To connect the diketone 36 with aldehyde 37.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond in the tetracyclic product

key_disconnections.reasoning: To establish the key chiral center in the early stage.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The synthesis of (+)-aplysiasecosterol A (6) features a highly efficient desymmetric enantioselective reduction of a 1,3-cyclopentanedione derivative as the key transformation to establish the initial stereocenter. Subsequent steps involve functional group manipulations, 2-bromoallylation, ketalization, oxidative dehydrogenation, Reformatsky-type radical addition, and HAT radical cyclization to construct the complex tetracyclic core.

thought.background: The target molecule is (+)-aplysiasecosterol A (6). The key strategy involves a desymmetric enantioselective reduction of a 1,3-cyclopentanedione derivative.
thought.gap: Need to synthesize the chiral building block from a symmetric precursor.
thought.decision: To prepare alcohol 30, chose CBS reduction conditions with (S)-29 as the catalyst, expecting satisfactory yield and ee value on multiple gram scales.

outcome.observation: The reaction proceeded smoothly on multiple gram scales with satisfactory yield and excellent enantioselectivity.

evidence.text_span: Their synthesis features a highly efficient desymmetric enantioselective reduction of diketone 28 for preparing alcohol 30 under the CBS conditions [8] with (S)-29 as the catalyst (Scheme 1) [14]. Notably, this reaction could be performed on multiple gram scales with satisfactory yield (72%) and ee value (92%).

thought.background: Alcohol 30 has been successfully synthesized with high yield and ee.
thought.gap: Need to protect the alcohol and modify the terminal double bond to prepare for subsequent steps.
thought.decision: To prepare ketoaldehyde 31, chose protection of the alcohol group with TBSCl followed by modification of the terminal double bond, expecting to afford the product.

outcome.observation: Ketoaldehyde 31 was afforded in high overall yield after three steps.

evidence.text_span: Protection of the alcohol group in 30 with TBSCl followed by modification of the terminal double bond afforded ketoaldehyde 31. The 2-bromoallylation [15] of 31 with boronic ester 32 stereoselectively constructed the C3–OH group to give homoallylic alcohol 33.

thought.background: Ketoaldehyde 31 is ready for further functionalization.
thought.gap: Need to stereoselectively construct the C3-OH group.
thought.decision: To construct the C3-OH group, chose 2-bromoallylation of 31 with boronic ester 32, expecting to give homoallylic alcohol 33 stereoselectively.

evidence.text_span: The 2-bromoallylation [15] of 31 with boronic ester 32 stereoselectively constructed the C3–OH group to give homoallylic alcohol 33.

thought.gap: Need to rapidly construct the bicyclic ketone 34.
thought.decision: To construct bicyclic ketone 34, chose successive manipulation by removal of TBS group, CSA-catalyzed ketalization, and DMP oxidation of the secondary alcohol to a ketone, expecting high overall yield.

outcome.observation: Bicyclic ketone 34 was constructed in high overall yield.

evidence.text_span: Next, a successive manipulation by removal of TBS group, CSA-catalyzed ketalization, and DMP oxidation of the secondary alcohol to a ketone allowed for rapid construction of bicyclic ketone 34 in high overall yield.

thought.gap: Need to introduce unsaturation into the bicyclic ketone.
thought.decision: To obtain α,β-unsaturated bicyclic ketone 35, chose oxidative dehydrogenation of 34, expecting it to proceed smoothly.

outcome.observation: α,β-unsaturated bicyclic ketone 35 was obtained smoothly.

evidence.text_span: The oxidative dehydrogenation of 34 gave α,β-unsaturated bicyclic ketone 35 smoothly.

thought.gap: Need to construct the A-ring and perform functional group modifications to obtain diketone 36.
thought.decision: To produce diketone 36, chose sequential A-ring construction and functional group modifications of 35, expecting a smooth transformation.

evidence.text_span: Sequential A-ring construction and functional group modifications of 35 produced the diketone 36.

thought.background: Diketone 36 and aldehyde 37 (prepared in 9 steps from (+)-citronellol) are available.
thought.gap: Need to connect these two fragments to form the aldol product.
thought.decision: To deliver the aldol product, chose a Reformatsky-type radical addition of 36 and 37 under Et3B/air/Bu3SnH conditions, expecting a successful addition.

outcome.observation: The aldol product was delivered. Subsequent dehydration gave (E)-38.

evidence.text_span: Subsequently, 36 and aldehyde 37, which was prepared with 9 steps from commercially available (+)-citronellol, underwent a Reformatsky-type radical addition under the conditions of Et3B/air/Bu3SnH to deliver aldol product [16]. Dehydration of the secondary alcohol gave (E)-38.

thought.gap: Need to form the tetracyclic product.
thought.decision: To furnish the tetracyclic product 39, chose HAT radical cyclization of 38 in the presence of Fe(dpm)3/Ph(iPrO)SiH2, expecting it to proceed smoothly.

outcome.observation: Tetracyclic product 39 was furnished in 56% yield with a 2.5:1 ratio.

evidence.text_span: The HAT radical cyclization [17] of 38 in the presence of Fe(dpm)3/Ph(iPrO)SiH2 proceeded smoothly to furnish the tetracyclic product 39 in 56% yield with 2.5:1 ratio.

thought.gap: Need to complete the total synthesis of (+)-aplysiasecosterol A (6).
thought.decision: To complete the total synthesis, chose removal of the acetonide and the Bn protecting group, expecting to yield the final product.

outcome.observation: The total synthesis of (+)-aplysiasecosterol A (6) was completed.

evidence.text_span: Finally, removal of the acetonide and the Bn protecting group completed the total synthesis of (+)-aplysiasecosterol A (6).

Overall Yield.interpretation: Overall yield not explicitly stated, but individual steps show good yields.

Longest Linear Steps.interpretation: The longest linear sequence of steps to synthesize the target.

validation.significance: First asymmetric total synthesis of (+)-aplysiasecosterol A, demonstrating the utility of desymmetric enantioselective reduction of 1,3-cyclopentanedione derivatives.

source.title: The application of desymmetric enantioselective reduction of cyclic 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds in the total synthesis of terpenoid and alkaloid natural products.
